message:
Last winter I was tracking a nasty ice storm headed our way, and I had my phone glued to my hand for weather updates. Then—dead battery. Just like that, mid-afternoon, I was cut off. Couldn't check the latest forecast, couldn't get alerts, couldn't even text my sister to make sure she was stocked up. Mild panic ensued.

Turned out fine, but it was such a good reminder: we lean *so hard* on our devices when bad weather rolls in, but they're about as reliable as milk in a power outage. Now I keep a little printed checklist of what we need to do *before* a storm hits—water, batteries, the obvious stuff—plus a battery-powered radio tucked away. Phone dies? No problem. We've still got what we need.

Do you keep any paper backups or non-tech tools on hand for when storms knock out power or reception?
